articles.title: Sri Lanka emerges as the new favourite for Indian destination weddings; average budgets hit ₹58 lakh domestically, ₹1.5 crore overseas
articles.summary: The WedMeGood Destination Wedding Report 2026 report reveals that destination wedding spending has risen 8% year-on-year, with the average domestic destination wedding budget now reaching ₹58 lakh while international celebrations command an even steeper invest…

articles.title: Retail inflation jumps to 4.38% in June, driven by food, jewellery prices; breaches RBI's 4% target
articles.summary: India's retail inflation increased to 4.38 percent in June from May's 3.93 percent. Higher food prices significantly pushed up the overall Consumer Price Index for the month. Ginger and tomatoes saw substantial year-on-year price increases, contributing to the overall rise.

articles.title: Jeff Bezos' ex-wife MacKenzie Scott gives $20 mn to youth mental health non-profit Active Minds
articles.summary: The unrestricted donation comes at a time when mental health challenges among American teenagers are intensifying, with nearly one in five adolescents reporting suicidal thoughts

articles.title: How small tweaks can save your gadgets from early death
articles.summary: Small habits can save your gadgets from early breakdowns. Cleaning, software updates, surge protection, and proper ventilation keep phones, laptops, printers, and appliances lasting longer.

articles.title: Terrifying Skywalks Around the World You Must See
articles.summary: Experience vertigo-inducing thrills at the world’s most terrifying skywalks, from Grand Canyon glass floors to Norway fjords, Iguazu Falls, China, and Swiss cliff-edge views.

articles.title: India's commerce portfolio: Imports outpace exports, trade deficit expands to $30.4 billion
articles.summary: India's merchandise imports surged thirty-one percent year-on-year in June. Exports also rose fifteen point five percent, while imports climbed sharply. The country's trade deficit stood at thirty point four three billion dollars for the month.

articles.title: The Moon is drifting away from Earth — and the reason starts in our oceans
articles.summary: The Moon is slowly moving away from Earth by about 3.8 cm every year. The reason is linked to ocean tides, Earth’s rotation and laser reflectors left by Apollo astronauts.

articles.title: 'Completely false and misleading': Piyush Goyal rebuts report of India holding up US trade pact
Why Minor Daily Actions Matter More Than Big Mistakes in Office Politics
articles.summary: In office politics, tiny daily habits often matter more than big mistakes. Micro-behaviors like tone, reactions, and attentiveness quietly shape respect, trust, and professional perception.

articles.title: Extreme Prices: These Global Destinations Will Blow Your Budget
articles.summary: Bermuda, Cayman, Switzerland, and other global hotspots top the charts for sky-high living costs. Luxury housing, imported goods, and exclusive locales make daily life a premium adventure.

articles.title: Wimbledon Greats: Who Holds the Most Men’s Singles Crowns?
articles.summary: Roger Federer holds the record with 8 Wimbledon men’s singles titles. Legends like Sampras and Djokovic follow with 7, while new stars like Jannik Sinner rise in modern tennis.

articles.title: Car mileage dropping suddenly? These everyday habits may be the reason
articles.summary: Low tyre pressure, AC use, idling and fuel quality can quietly reduce car mileage. Here are 6 everyday reasons your car may be consuming more fuel than expected.

articles.title: Supreme Court: Bank merger without landlord's consent can attract eviction under Delhi Rent Control Act
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that a bank cannot resist eviction under the Delhi Rent Control Act merely because it came into possession of leased premises through a statutory amalgamation.

articles.title: Purpose over privilege: Why this Swiss woman left Dubai boardrooms to educate underprivileged kids in India
articles.summary: Her journey eventually brought her to Sagar Island, where she initially hoped to work with existing non-profit organisations. However, after spending time on the ground, she felt many grassroots initiatives struggled with transparency or failed to create lasting impact.

articles.title: HDFC Bank's automation & AI push cuts over 3,300 job in FY26
articles.summary: Non-supervisory employees fell by more than 8,000 to 162,797, indicating that a significant part of the reduction may have been in operational and back-office roles

articles.title: Found old ₹2000 notes in your cupboard? RBI says you can still exchange them — here's how
articles.summary: RBI reminds Indians that ₹2000 notes are still legal tender and can be exchanged at 19 regional offices or sent by post. Here's the complete step-by-step process to return them now.

articles.title: Spain's Submer to invest $2 billion in Madhya Pradesh semiconductor sector, may create 5,000 jobs
articles.summary: Spain's Submer Group will invest two billion dollars in Madhya Pradesh's semiconductor sector. This significant investment is expected to generate approximately five thousand direct employment opportunities.

articles.title: 1.27 crore railway bedsheets, towels, blankets stolen since 2022. Here's what an RTI investigation found
articles.summary: According to RTI data, roughly one in every 1,000 AC passengers walks away with at least one bedroll item at the end of their journey.

articles.title: Meet Ramdulal Deb: The Bengali trader who shaped US-India commerce in 18th century
articles.summary: Perhaps the greatest tribute came from Salem's merchant community. An American merchant vessel was christened Ram Dolloll, reflecting the esteem in which he was held by traders whose fortunes were increasingly tied to commerce with India.

articles.title: Policybazaar’s hospital entry: Can PB Health connect care, cover and tech?
articles.summary: PB Fintech has opened its first hospital in Gurugram as it seeks to bring medical care, insurance and technology together under the PB Health brand.

articles.title: Government clears Pranab Mukherjee memorial at Rajghat's Rashtriya Smriti complex
articles.summary: The memorial will be built at the Rashtriya Smriti Sthal, a designated area within the Rajghat complex reserved for commemorating former Presidents, Vice Presidents and Prime Ministers

articles.title: Kospi dream rally fumbles: World's best-performing stock plunges 7% - what's behind crash?
articles.summary: South Korea's Kospi index experienced a significant 7% decline on Monday. Rising Middle East tensions and oil price spikes rattled global investors worldwide. Technology stocks, including SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics, led the sharp fall.

articles.title: Nifty Realty Index: How NSE measures performance of real estate sector
articles.summary: The Nifty Realty Index tracks India's listed real estate companies' performance. It comprises ten firms selected by free-float market capitalization and rebalanced semi-annually. DLF, Phoenix Mills, and Lodha Developers hold the largest weights within the index.

articles.title: Chaos returns to Middle East: Oil prices jump 4% as US-Iran exchange strikes
articles.summary: Oil prices surged sharply as renewed military action between Iran and the United States escalated tensions. Fresh strikes expanded to Qatar and the United Arab Emirates over the weekend. This escalation has clouded the future of the interim US-Iran agreement signed last month.

articles.title: Quote of the Day by Rahul Bajaj: ‘We are a true, proud and an independent nation...’
articles.summary: As the long-time chairman of the Bajaj Group, he became a household name across India for putting the middle class on wheels and helping shape the country's modern economic landscape.

articles.title: Auto lenders bet on embedded finance
articles.summary: Auto financiers are pushing for embedded finance and digital credit integration. This shift aims to make car purchases more seamless for customers. Traditional installment loans still dominate the Indian auto finance market.

articles.title: After two years, pharma volumes pick up pace
articles.summary: Domestic pharmaceutical retail growth shows strong signs of demand-led expansion. Volume growth in June exceeded three percent, a significant positive shift. Industry growth outlook is now revised upwards to over eleven percent this year.

articles.title: Government weighs cybersecurity framework for IoT devices
articles.summary: India is considering a cybersecurity framework for internet-connected devices. This initiative aims to enhance security standards for smart meters and other IoT products. The government is exploring measures for stricter security and certification requirements.

articles.title: Foreign investors return to India, equities lead revival
articles.summary: Foreign investors have returned to Indian markets, showing renewed interest in equities during July. This shift follows months of consistent selling, with significant inflows recorded in early July.

articles.title: Oil companies plan expanding 10-kg LPG cylinder sales
articles.summary: State-run oil companies are considering expanding lightweight 10-kg LPG cylinders for commercial use. These cylinders will likely be sold at commercial LPG rates if approved by authorities. This move could benefit migrant workers, tenants, and street vendors needing LPG.

articles.title: NFL's biggest-ever deal: India-born billionaire Vinod Khosla buys Seattle Seahawks for $9.6 billion
articles.summary: The Seahawks' sale eclipses the previous NFL record of $6.05 billion paid for the Washington Commanders in 2023. At nearly $9.6 billion, the transaction underlines how professional sports teams have evolved into premium global assets that attract long-term inv…

articles.title: Four top-10 firms add Rs 93,000 crore in mcap despite weak market; HDFC Bank, Airtel lead gains
articles.summary: Four of India's top ten companies added significant market value last week. HDFC Bank and Bharti Airtel led these gains, increasing their valuations substantially. Reliance Industries also saw its market capitalization rise, retaining its top position.
